title: cuscuta mantiqueirana
intro: Cuscuta mantiqueirana, also known as the dodder vine, is a parasitic plant that derives its nutrients from other plants, specifically the Capparaceae family, by connecting to them through specialized haustoria. Traditionally, Cuscuta mantiqueirana has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits associated with Cuscuta mantiqueirana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antiviral properties. Cuscuta mantiqueirana can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in some regions of Brazil.
